text.content:
Chapter 2 — Seven Days to the Grave. Korvosa quiets after the riots — and then Queen Ileosa's true horror begins: a manufactured plague, Blood Veil, loosed to cull the citizens she despises. The PCs (now ~L4) race to find a cure as the city dies around them.

The conspiracy (GM only). Ileosa always meant her husband's murder to be only the start. Through the Red Mantis assassin Kayltanya she obtained Vorel's phage; the Mantis connected her to the 'gentleman's killer' Dr. Reiner Davaulus, who with the Urgathoan zealot Lady Andaisin and the vampire-scholar Ramoska Arkminos weaponized the phage into the fast, dramatic, terror-spreading blood veil.

The delivery. A fortune in Korvosan silver was tainted with blood veil using a death's head coffer and scattered through the poor quarters and the Bank of Abadar — striking the very priests best placed to fight it. As cover, the conspirators sank the plague-ship Direption in the Jeggare as a red herring: investigators who chase the ship waste days while the tainted coin spreads. Letting blood veil run its course cripples the city's will to resist Ileosa's deeper pacts.

This is the payoff of the Blood Veil setup from Chapter 1. Run the persistent-plague overlay from here (Conversion Guide journal → Blood Veil & the Epidemic Clock): start the Epidemic Clock now, seeded by how the city fared in 'Edge of Anarchy.'

Advancement. The PCs begin well into 4th level → 5th after their first plague mission → 6th before Carowyn Manor / Racker's Alley → 7th before the Temple of Urgathoa beneath the hospice → just 8th by the chapter's end.

The seven days. Blood veil takes ~7 days to kill, so the early deaths are isolated among the poor and go unnoticed for days. Keep the PCs busy first (the most time-consuming early task is smuggling Trinia Sabor out of the city), and give them time to resupply and form attachments — the coming tragedies should land personally.

The Second Harrowing. Zellara's deck performs its second reading at the chapter's start, generating this chapter's Harrow Points (Conversion Guide → The Harrowing). This chapter is tied to the suit of shields and to Constitution — read the cards grim: mass graves, ashen-faced women (the Gray Maidens), carrion birds (the Queen's Physicians).

Threads from Chapter 1 continue even where the NPCs have no direct role here:

  • Amin Jalento barricades himself in his Old Korvosa home, survives the plague, and resurfaces in Chapter 3.
  • Devargo Barvasi locks down Eel's End to ride out the quarantine — but unless the PCs intervened, his ettercap @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.chittersnap00001]{Chittersnap}, starving, turns on and kills him (his silk-wrapped body found in the hold). If Chittersnap died in Chapter 1, Devargo survives and Eel's End can be a safe haven.
  • Neolandus Kalepopolis, the missing seneschal hiding in Old Korvosa, is captured by the Arkonas as this chapter opens — the hook into Chapter 3.

Within hours of Trinia's botched execution, Field Marshal Cressida Kroft seeks out the PCs, worried. Queen Ileosa is restructuring Korvosa's military; Cressida fears for her own position and warns the PCs that her ability to fund their work — and to shield them — is shrinking.

Mechanically: the PCs should no longer consider themselves official Korvosan Guard agents. Cressida still aids them where she can and asks where to reach them — establishing her as their fraying but loyal patron as the city tilts toward martial law.

Sabina Merrin first appeared in gleaming red-and-crimson full plate at Trinia's near-execution. She now commands the Gray Maidens — an all-women elite loyal only to Ileosa, handed the Longacre Building (arbiter Zenobia Zenderholm welcomes them; the bound efreeti Yzahnum and the bloatmage seneschal Togomor speed their arming) as their headquarters.

The truth. Recruits are chosen for beauty and strength; those who fail are disfigured and imprisoned in the dungeons below. Those who pass endure a breaking, scarring initiation. Present them at first as saviors of order — the personification of martial law — and let their brutal nature surface across the chapter. Once they appear, Ileosa retreats into the sealed Castle until Chapter 3.

Use @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden Foot Soldier} for rank-and-file Gray Maidens in this and later chapters.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A Damsel in Distress — p.76.

Vencarlo Orisini (secretly Blackjack, who rescued Trinia from the gallows) sends the PCs a cryptic note inviting them to his Old Korvosa academy at 16 Hillcrest Street.

My friends, I hope this note finds you in good health in these dangerous times. In light of the recent unrest, I feel that I could provide some tips on self-defense to adventurers such as you, and would be most gratified to meet with you for these lessons, offered gratis, at Orisini Academy, 16 Hillcrest Street. I eagerly await your arrival.

Vencarlo Orisini HANDOUT #2-1 no problem for the well-connected swordmaster, but in the days following the city riots and general upheaval, many of Vencarlo's usual contacts are either doing time or lying low. Further, he knows he's being watched by the government, and he can't disrupt his routine. Yet every day Trinia remains hidden in his home is another she might be discovered.

Thus, he turns to the adventurers whom his friend Field Marshal Kroft put so much faith in and sends them a short, cryptic note that invites them to pay him a visit in his academy in Old Korvosa (see Handout #2-1).

Inside, he reveals @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.triniaSabor00001]{Trinia Sabor} in disguise and asks the PCs to smuggle her to safety — to a wayside inn called Trots (12 miles north), where Jasan Adriel takes her on to a ranch in Harse. He funds the trip (a horse, supplies, 20 gp for the inn) and, if pressed, offers 500 gp.

A subtle egress is best — a low-key walk out the gates among the afternoon merchants. Crimson-tunic'd guards, Hellknights, mercenaries, and the odd Gray Maiden eye the party, but pass unless the PCs do something suspicious (showy magic, a fight). Optional hinterland encounters en route let Trinia (a bard) fight beside them toward 5th level.

GM: Ileosa has set a 5,000 gp bounty on Trinia. Trinia strongly suspects Vencarlo is Blackjack but keeps it to herself; she warms to the PCs and may become a friend (she returns in Chapter 4).

Rewards: deliver Trinia to Trots → 2,400 XP. Befriended, she gifts the party her masterwork mithral dagger and her wand of daze monster ('I won't need them in Harse — you might').

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Yellow Lights / First Symptoms — p.80.

One night early in the chapter (ideally while the PCs are escorting Trinia), the queen's red herring sails in: the Direption, a black-sailed Urgathoan ship hung with yellow quarantine lanterns, before it sinks in the Jeggare. Anyone hunting the plague's source is meant to waste days on the wreck — and meet the deadly thing the cult left in it (the Direption wreck, built in the next increment).

A second favor. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grauSoldado00001]{Grau Soldado} — sober and back with the Guard — begs the PCs' help: his niece Brienna (in Trail's End) has broken out in red pocks, can't keep food down, and is failing. At the Soldado home the PCs meet the Abadaran healer @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani Dhatri}, brewing herbs he can't quite make work — he doesn't recognize Brienna's symptoms, and fears a new disease.

Saving Brienna: she has Blood Veil (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained} from Con loss). Remove disease cures it; lesser restoration staves off the drain. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani} will sell a remove disease potion or return to cast it for 150 gp — or, with @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:12] (he's ashamed to charge; @Check[type:perception|dc:20] reads it), cast it free the next day.

Exposure. Treating Brienna exposes the PCs and Ishani to Blood Veil — each attempts @Check[type:fortitude|dc:18] or contracts it (the staged disease in the Conversion Guide). This is the plague's first on-screen case: start the Epidemic Clock now (Conversion Guide → Blood Veil & the Epidemic Clock).

Story award: cure Brienna → 1,600 XP. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani} — impressed — offers to aid the PCs' charitable work and can be reached at the Grand Vault of Abadar; befriended, he heals them free thereafter.

pages.name: The Unwashed Masses & the Missing Arbiter

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Outbreak / Grand Vault — p.82.

Ishani leads the PCs to the Grand Vault of Abadar (North Point), seat of Archbanker Darb Tuttle and the city's most powerful faith — and, by Ileosa's design, the institution best placed to fight the plague and so the one her tainted silver strikes first.

Yet for a deity of law, the steep stairs and ramps leading up to the temple's great bronze doors offer a strangely discordant scene. Dozens of citizens-mostly of the working class, although the silks of a few merchants show through the crowd-throng the entry, scarcely being held back by a group of gold-armored Abadaran clerics. All seem intent on gaining entry to the temple, but the clerics turn away nearly all comers. The clerics' reasoning becomes clear as one desperate believer is turned away, his pitiful countenance mottled with violent red sores.

To reach the doors the PCs must shove through the desperate crowd — @Check[type:athletics|dc:18] each. Any obvious healer is mobbed for aid. Ishani's name (or his company) gets them past the door guards.

The Missing Arbiter. Inside, the plague's spread is undeniable and the trail of the missing investigating arbiter opens the cure-hunt. Field Marshal Kroft soon calls the PCs to a meeting (First, Do No Harm) to formally task them with finding the source.

Story award: navigate the crowd without harming anyone → 1,600 XP.

From here the chapter opens into the cure missions (the Direption wreck, Carowyn Manor, Racker's Alley) and finally the Hospice of the Blessed Maiden / Temple of Urgathoa — built in the next increments. Advance the Epidemic Clock as in-city days pass.

pages.name: First, Do No Harm & the Queen's Physicians

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, First, Do No Harm — p.85.

Field Marshal Kroft summons the PCs to Citadel Volshyenek. Royal orders have just arrived: the Guard must escort and obey Queen Ileosa's new Queen's Physicians and treat any Gray Maiden's command as a superior officer's.

The echoes of forcefully spoken but unintelligible words resound off the imposing granite and iron walls of Citadel Volshyenek's outer curtain. Dozens of red-and-silverarmored guards stand in assembly upon the pitted stone mustering ground here, muttering in hushed, somber tones. Before them, atop a weathered wooden platform, paces Field Marshal Kroft, her eyebrows arched sternly as she momentarily tolerates the crowd's murmurs. Behind her upon the scaffold stand three grizzled veteran guards at attention, as well as an ominous-looking group.

These newcomers wear cowled robes of oily-looking leather, supple gloves, and wide black hats. Some grip heavy canes, others dark satchels. Each of them, though, wears a dark-goggled mask tapering to a pointed beak. Among them stand two others. The first is a middle-aged gentleman in a simple black overcoat with streaks of white gracing the sides of his short, dark hair. He watches the gathered guards with a soft, concerned expression, his hands tightly clasping a heavylooking doctor's case.

The second figure is an imposing one a few moments to quietly gripe about their new orders. The armored woman is Kordaitra Destaid, the quartermaster and second-in-command of the Gray Maidens. Although she does not remove her helm and takes no notice of the PCs at this time, she confronts them more directly later in this adventure. Kroft and her veteran attendants begin to head into the citadel with Dr. Davaulus and his Queen's Physicians, but PCs who act fast can easily catch the commander's attention.

Even if they don't try to catch up with Kroft, the field marshal notices them before she enters the keep and sends one of her aides to fetch the PCs. The leader of the Korvosan Guard welcomes the PCs and is even eager to see them, but she seems guarded in her current company; Davaulus's doctors unnerve her just as much as they do her guards. She introduces the PCs to Dr. Davaulus, leader of the Queen's Physicians. A calm man with a polite demeanor and deeply analytical mind, Dr.

Davaulus greets the PCs warmly and welcomes their questions, saying he hopes to ease the concerns of as many of Korvosa's people as possible in this trying time. If questioned about his plans to help Korvosa, Davaulus admits he must still confer with Field Marshal Kroft to form a sensible plan, but produces a public announcement from his bag-one of numerous such writs soon to be distributed throughout the city (see Handout #2-2 on page 87)-to convey the queen's initial expectations.

Kroft introduces Dr. Reiner Davaulus, leader of the Queen's Physicians — calm, fatherly, analytical — and hands out the royal decree (Handout #2-2). The Gray Maiden is Kordaitra Destaid, the Maidens' quartermaster, who will confront the PCs later. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani} cautiously offers to coordinate the Abadaran effort.

By Decree of Her Royal Majesty, the Radiant Queen Ileosa, all citizens and members of the Korvosan Guard are to aid and admit the newly established Queen's Physicians in this time of urgency. These royal agents will extend healing to those who are ill and organize defense against the spreading affliction known as 'blood veil.' They are to be allowed access to any home or building they deem necessary in the course of their duties. All those suffering from disease or disorder are to submit themselves to the Physicians for treatment.

The truth. Davaulus is a Red Mantis assassin — a poisoner who masterminded blood veil with Andaisin and Ramoska; his masked 'physicians' are @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Urgathoa cultists} who spread the plague while feigning to treat it. His one tell: he praises the queen too warmly. He is the Hospice finale boss (Part 3).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Combating the Plague — p.88.

Kroft (and Davaulus, for show) sets the PCs to investigate the plague's source and stem it. The chapter offers several missions, run in any order over the days the city sickens — each advances the Epidemic Clock as time passes. The headline lead is the plague-ship sunk in the Jeggare (Mission 1), with Carowyn Manor and Racker's Alley to follow.

The Quarantine of Old Korvosa. After the PCs complete two or three missions (and before the Hospice in Part 3), Ileosa quarantines all of Old Korvosa: the Gray Maidens burn every wooden bridge and barricade the one stone bridge. This cannot be prevented — it sets up Chapter 3. (How to come and go appears in Chapter 3.)

pages.name: Mission 1: The Wreck of the Direption

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 1 — the Direption — p.89.

Cressida gives the wreck's location. Diving the muddy Jeggare, @Check[type:perception|dc:15] locates the ship (@Check[type:perception|dc:25] without a light source); @Check[type:athletics|dc:10] swims the placid deep. Every 10 minutes searching carries a cumulative chance of drawing the silt eels. @Check[type:society|dc:20] identifies a Nidalese merchantman; @Check[type:society|dc:30] (Guard records) traces it to the long-'lost' Direption of Garagori Whenston — a dead-end that is precisely the point.

It is a red herring. The ship carries no plague source — only clues that waste days. Lady Andaisin left a lethal surprise: the sea-hag druid @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca} and her jigsaw-shark companion Skinshear, moved into the wreck to kill investigators. The hull lies split in two over a rock; the stern's main hatch (@Check[type:athletics|dc:26]) and hold trap door (@Check[type:athletics|dc:23]) are swollen shut, but a 10-ft trebuchet hole opens easy access to A2.

This is an underwater dungeon — without water-breathing/freedom of movement the PCs fight at a real disadvantage; flag that to the GM. Re-judge attack/movement penalties per the PF2e aquatic-combat rules.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A1 — p.90.

The shattered bow lies open to the river. Six @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.siltEel000000001]{silt eels} nest in the silt among the splinters, lunging at anything that disturbs them.

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.siltEel000000001]{Silt Eel (−1)}
Silt eel nestTrivial · 0 XP @ L4

6 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.siltEel000000001]{silt eels} swarm — individually trivial, but their stacking venom and the underwater footing make them a real nuisance.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A2 — p.90.

The trebuchet hole opens into the flooded hold. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca} has set her jigsaw-shark companion Skinshear here to guard the way down. It circles, butts the door to A3 to warn its mistress, then attacks.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Skinshear — Jigsaw Shark (1, elite)}
Skinshear (Yvicca's shark)Trivial · 15 XP @ L4

Skinshear — an unusually large @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{jigsaw shark} (the GM may apply the Elite adjustment for the named companion). It fights to the death and alerts Yvicca.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, A3 — p.91.

The cleaved stern holds the captain's cabin — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca's} lair. She prepares (Evil Eye, then joins from A2 once Skinshear raises the alarm) and fights as the wreck's boss.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca (6)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Skinshear (1)}
BOSS — YviccaModerate · 80 XP @ L4

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.yviccaSeaHag0001]{Yvicca}, a sea-hag druid (with Skinshear if it still lives). Underwater, at range, with Horrific Appearance and Tanglevine, she is a genuine Severe-leaning threat for a party that came unprepared.

Yvicca's hoard (re-scaled to PF2e ~L5–6): a few hundred gp in coin and pearls, a minor druidic trinket, and the cult's planted clues (forged manifests pointing nowhere) that confirm the wreck was bait. No plague source is here.

Clearing the wreck proves the plague's source lies elsewhere — pushing the PCs toward Carowyn Manor / Racker's Alley and, ultimately, the Hospice.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 2 — Plague Rats — p.92.

Plague-panicked mobs have begun lynching Korvosa's reclusive wererats, and the firebrand Girrigz Ripperclaws answers with a call to war on the city above. An elder wererat, Eries Yelloweyes (50 years hidden as a fishmonger), fears a purge and approaches the PCs in human form: stop Girrigz — ideally without a slaughter of her people — and she'll have her kin fight the plague from the shadows (disposing of infected bodies). She gives directions to his lair beneath Midland.

The sewers are slick: moving faster than half Speed or attacking in the sewage flow forces @Check[type:acrobatics|dc:12] or fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. A @Check[type:nature|dc:15] recognizes the alarm-fungus shrieker in B2.

Survivor Count: scattering Girrigz's warband without killing the other (coerced) wererats saves 400 citizens and seeds an ongoing wererat ally network — a major win for the persistent-plague Epidemic Clock.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B1 — p.92.

The approach to Girrigz's camp through Korvosa's sewers (pad it with wandering-monster encounters if the party needs XP). The main tunnel bends east to a rusty grate overlooking B4.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B2 — p.92.

A guard post around a sewage pool. A shrieker fungus by the south wall screeches if any creature enters and doesn't immediately move north past the central pillar — its noise summons the B3 wererats and the B5 swarm, and wakes the B4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh}.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{Wererat (2)}
  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{Giant Rat (−1)}
Wererat guardsTrivial · 30 XP @ L5

2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{wererats} and 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{giant rats} (the rats avoid crossing the water lest they trip the shrieker; the wererats cross it deliberately to raise the alarm).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B3 — p.93.

The wererats' fire-lit warren of nests. The floor is slippery in the sewage flow (as B2). @Check[type:perception|dc:20] finds a squeeze-hole to B6; @Check[type:crafting|dc:14] notes the subsiding south wall — chipping it (Hardness 8, 40 HP) lets the trapped @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh} rampage through.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{Wererat (2)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz (6, reinforces)}
Wererat den (Girrigz reinforces)Low · 60 XP @ L5

4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2k0JX3RFTVRf0KS2]{wererats}. If fighting erupts here, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz} comes through the east wall from B6 in his dire-rat form.

@Check[type:perception|dc:18]: 3 smokesticks, a tanglefoot bag, a thunderstone, 20 tindertwigs, a copper coat-of-arms trumpet (~15 gp re-scaled), and masterwork carpenter's tools.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B4 — p.93.

The wererats have penned a lone @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh} between two grates (Hardness 10, 60 HP, lift @Check[type:athletics|dc:26]; the jammed east-grate winch retracts it after a minute's cranking). Starving, it sleeps until noise wakes it.

The otyugh ('so hungeries!') can be turned against the wererats: @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:23] (+4 if fed) wins an hour's help, or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:17] buys 10 minutes — after which it forgets and tries to eat the PCs.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{Otyugh (4)}
Otyugh (if it attacks)Trivial · 30 XP @ L5

One hungry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyugh} — a potential ally or a third combatant in the wererat fight.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B5 — p.93.

A bone-littered nest cave. It attacks anything that doesn't smell of rodent; the swarm investigates if the shrieker sounds, but the rats are too lazy to.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.6wPW2dvpt86Ou6bL]{Rat Swarm (1)}
  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{Giant Rat (−1)}
Rat nestTrivial · 10 XP @ L5

A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.6wPW2dvpt86Ou6bL]{rat swarm} and 6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.iIJPJcDT8wlJ8z5M]{giant rats}.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, B6 — p.94.

The warlord's command den — a crude map of Korvosa pinned to a crate, crates of scavenged arms and alchemist's fire. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz} plots his war here, dreaming of burning Castle Korvosa.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz Ripperclaws (6)}
BOSS — Girrigz RipperclawsLow · 60 XP @ L5

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.girrigzRipper001]{Girrigz} — pre-buffed if warned, striking from stealth with his runed rapier and Vital Strike. He fights to the death.

@Check[type:perception|dc:15]: an arms cache (22 daggers, 12 short swords, 3 crossbows, 4 chain shirts, 12 alchemist's fire, a masterwork longsword + chainmail). @Check[type:perception|dc:20] in the nest: 4 potions of healing, a bottle of air, and a pearl of power (1st-rank) — plus the squeeze-crack to B3.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 3 — The Color of Death — p.96.

The Lavender perfumery off Summoning Street, owned by the Chelish con-artist @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra Loaggri}, is selling 'Lavender's Luxuriant Liniment' — a 2-gp 'plague cure' that is sugar, cheap scent, and river water. By day a queue stretches four blocks; the mission is to expose the fraud, not to fight.

Cracking the con: @Check[type:crafting|dc:22] (an hour with an alchemist's lab) reveals the liniment is worthless; @Check[type:perception|dc:18] spots her planted shill Solt Carmino (the only 'customer' browsing perfume), and @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:25] or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:25] makes him confess. The cleanest proof: administer a dose to a real plague victim before a Korvosan Guard (Grau or Kroft will witness), then actually cure them.

This 'cure' is a dead end for the plague (and a moral test) — it heals no one. Watching Vendra's thugs haul river-water barrels from the Jeggare by night (easy to tail) is the fastest evidence. Report to Cressida and the Guard arrests her.

Story award: expose the scam and shut Lavender down → 1,600 XP, plus full XP for everyone (Vendra included) the PCs didn't have to kill.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, C1 — p.96.

A cramped, stylish shop of amethyst glass, its counter stacked with magenta-stoppered phials of the fake liniment. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra} works the crowd while two shop-girls sell and two @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender thugs} watch with saps. Confront her with proof and she screams for the PCs' ejection; cornered, she flees toward C2, knifing a PC on the way out.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra Loaggri (5)}
  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender Thug (3)}
Vendra + thugs (by day)Low · 60 XP @ L6

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vendraLoaggri001]{Vendra} and 2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender thugs} amid scattering customers (CR7-equivalent by day; fewer guards and no crowd at night make it easier). Vendra fights dirty and runs.

@Check[type:society|dc:18] picks out genuine perfumes worth ~80 gp re-scaled; the counter box holds ~40 gp in recent sales, and the safe (@Check[type:thievery|dc:28]) a few hundred gp in coin + three lapis perfume bottles. Vendra carries a circlet of persuasion, a wand of charm person, and a wand of remove disease.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, C2 — p.99.

A locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:20]), prim parody of a Chelish noble's salon — no alchemy here. @Check[type:perception|dc:20] finds a corner-hinged secret door into the boarded-up lab (C3). By night there's a 50% chance Vendra sleeps here; otherwise she's mixing brew in C3.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, C3 — p.99.

The 'condemned' apartment behind the shop, boarded shut inside and out (Hardness 5, 15 HP, @Check[type:athletics|dc:24] to break), reachable by the C2 secret door. Here the fake cure is mixed from river water hauled in nightly. By day one @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{thug} works; by night Vendra and two thugs brew.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender Thug (3)}
Lab guardsTrivial · 15 XP @ L6

1 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lavenderThug0001]{Lavender thug} by day (more at night with Vendra).

The lab equipment functions as an alchemist's lab; the raw ingredients are ~800 gp (PF1e) of potion/alchemy reagents — re-scale to a useful crafting stock for the party.

Mission complete: the perfumery con collapses. It cured nothing — the real source is still out there, pointing the PCs onward (Racker's Alley, Carowyn Manor, the Hospice).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 4 — The Hungry Dead — p.100.

As bodies pile up, lazy plague-carters have been dumping corpses in Racker's Alley (western Old Korvosa) instead of the Gray District. Kroft (or the temple, or rumor) sends the PCs to confirm it and clear any scavengers or undead before guards haul the dead away.

GM: four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} — minions of the Ustalavic vampire-count Tiriac, sent to 'assist' (and watch) the nosferatu Ramoska Arkminos — have lairred under Giotorri's Toys and feed on the dumped (and the living). They long ago forgot their charge to watch Ramoska. Ramoska is the Temple-of-Urgathoa villain (Part 3).

Survivor Count: clearing the spawn and confirming the dumping ground (so the Guard can stop it) saves 200 citizens — feed it into the Epidemic Clock.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, D1 — p.100.

A shadowed, sunless dead-end alley with 40+ corpses heaped against the east wall. @Check[type:perception|dc:16] notes that many bodies are blood-drained with neck/wrist punctures — and bear no blood-veil sores. Contact with the plague-dead exposes a creature to Blood Veil (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:18]). A 10-ft hole in the east wall opens into the workshop (D2).

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{Vampire Spawn (4)}
Vampire spawn sentryTrivial · 20 XP @ L6

By night one @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} watches from the 20-ft roof (−2 to spot it from the alley floor). Seeing anyone touch the bodies, it shrieks and attacks; its 3 companions arrive from D2 in 1d3 rounds.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, D2 — p.101.

Beneath the toy-shop: the lair where the four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} keep their coffins, watched over by the desiccated corpse of the toymaker Rodolfo Giotorri (eight neck-punctures; @Check[type:perception|dc:15] finds his key ring). Fight all four here if the alley alarm was raised.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{Vampire Spawn (4)}
BOSS — the four vampire spawnModerate · 80 XP @ L6

All 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.dFzTXkrpoOOdbzuW]{vampire spawn} — blood drain, dominate gaze, drain, fast healing, and their vampire weaknesses (running water, sunlight). A genuine Severe-leaning fight; bait them toward sunlight or a threshold.

@Check[type:perception|dc:20] in the coffin soil: ~15 pp + 300 gp of Ustalavic coin (re-scale to ~90 gp), a ring of jumping (brass spider-legs), and pipes of haunting (bone).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, D3 — p.101.

The dusty, locked showroom above (@Check[type:thievery|dc:30], or Giotorri's key from D2). The spawn left the cheap, defective toys alone.

@Check[type:perception|dc:14]: a jingling lockbox (@Check[type:thievery|dc:20]) with a few coins and an Abadaran deposit-box key (No. 261; @Check[type:society|dc:16] or @Check[type:religion|dc:16] to place it). Redeemed at the Grand Vault, it yields Giotorri's savings (~68 gp), masterwork thieves' tools, and boots of striding and springing.

Clearing Racker's Alley removes one of Ramoska's loose ends — and quietly establishes the vampire's presence ahead of the Temple finale.

pages.name: Mission 5: The Case of the Vanishing Virtuoso

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Mission 5 — Carowyn Manor — p.104.

Deyanira Mirukova begs the PCs (or Kroft refers her) to find her brother Ruan, a Varisian ocarina prodigy who went to perform at a masquerade at Carowyn Manor and never returned. The estate now sits locked, curtained, and reeking. @Check[type:society|dc:16] recognizes Ruan as a genuine virtuoso. (Her reward is meager — heirlooms under 100 gp and Marbledome passes — but the trail matters.)

GM: Ileosa added rich undesirables to the plague's toll; Davaulus farmed the hit-list to the necromancer Rolth Lamm (Gaedren's son, the absent master of the Ch.1 Dead Warrens), who — absorbed in studying Varisian plague-immunity — handed it to his lover @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina Susperio}. She murdered the entire party, raised 21 of the guests as zombies, and disguised every corpse as a blood-veil death. The 'plague' here is staged.

Getting in: the mahogany front doors are locked (@Check[type:athletics|dc:26] / @Check[type:thievery|dc:30]); the balcony banisters take @Check[type:athletics|dc:15] to climb; the spiked garden hedge @Check[type:athletics|dc:12] (fail by 5+ → @Damage[1d6[piercing]]). Jolistina jammed the side doors and shutters (@Check[type:athletics|dc:25]/@Check[type:thievery|dc:22] doors; shutters easier).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, E1 — p.108.

Regardless of how they make it into Carowyn Manor, the PCs should immediately realize that something is terribly wrong. The masquerade that was supposed to be occurring here is most obviously over: every papier-mache mask and sequined gown hides a blister-covered corpse. With merely two exceptions-Jolistina Susperio and Ausio Carowyn-every person in Carowyn Manor is dead, killed by the murderous elf.

The whole manor is one complex encounter. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina} hides and plays cat-and-mouse, ordering her posed zombies to defend her while she snipes and Vanishes. Of her 21 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies}, 7 are uncontrolled (they attack anyone).

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1a Great Hall — dancing deadTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{human zombies} posed as dancing noble couples; they turn and attack 1 round after the PCs enter.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1b Den — uncontrolled trioTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

3 uncontrolled @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies} (lion/lioness/peacock masks); Jolistina avoids this room.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{Human Zombie (−1)}
E1c Dining Room — dinersTrivial · 0 XP @ L6

4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies} mechanically 'dining' among real corpses.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, E2 — p.110.

More posed @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies} (the remaining ~8) drift through the bedrooms and salon. Here too is the surviving host, Ausio Carowyn — broken and captive, kept alive as Jolistina's plaything — and, among the masquerade dead, Deyanira's brother Ruan.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina Susperio (8)}
  • + remaining @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombies}
BOSS — Jolistina SusperioModerate · 80 XP @ L6

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina} makes her stand (or her endless retreat) here, fighting from behind whatever zombies remain. Run her as the manor-spanning skirmish she is, not a stand-up fight.

Jolistina carries 2 potions of invisibility, a potion of remove disease, wands of animate dead / sculpt corpse / cat's grace, +1 glamered studded leather, a hand crossbow with 3 screaming bolts, and ~150 gp + stolen jewelry. The manor itself holds a noble household's worth of re-scaled valuables.

The reveal: exposing the staged deaths proves the nobles were murdered, not plagued — and the wand of sculpt corpse + Jolistina's link to Rolth ties the plague to the necromancer and the cult of Urgathoa, pointing toward the Hospice. Save Ausio; recover Ruan's body for Deyanira.

pages.name: Part 3 — Epidemic: The Hospice of the Blessed Maiden

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Hospice — p.112.

Part 3 — Epidemic. The trail converges on the Hospice of the Blessed Maiden, the Queen's Physicians' headquarters in a converted warehouse — and on the hidden Temple of Urgathoa beneath it. The PCs should be 7th level entering the temple, and just 8th by the chapter's end. This is the plague's heart: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Davaulus}, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinUrgath01]{Andaisin}, and the nosferatu @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska}.

The hospice masquerades as a legitimate plague-treatment center, patrolled by @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} and the masked @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physicians}. Bluffing or sneaking in beats fighting through; pushing too hard gets the PCs an audience with Davaulus, who tries to misdirect them onto a fictional wererat cult.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F1 — p.112.

The warehouse front: a façade of cots, suffering patients, and brisk masked physicians. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} control access; a @Check[type:deception|dc:24] or genuine business gets the PCs past the entry without a fight.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden (3)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physician (2)}
Entry watchTrivial · 10 XP @ L7

A @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden} and a @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physician} on the door (more respond to an alarm).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F5 — p.113.

Catwalks ring the warehouse's upper level over the treatment floor — the physicians' route between wards. Gray Maidens patrol here.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maiden (3)}
Catwalk patrolTrivial · 20 XP @ L7

2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenFoot01]{Gray Maidens} — sword-and-shield in the choke of the catwalk, longbows if the PCs hang back.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F7 — p.114.

Behind the public wards, the physicians 'treat' captured plague victims — in truth refining and spreading blood veil. The horror here is hard proof of the conspiracy.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physician (2)}
Physicians at workTrivial · 0 XP @ L7

3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queensPhysician1]{Queen's Physicians}, flanking in silence for Sneak Attacks.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, F8 — p.116.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Dr. Reiner Davaulus}'s office, and the concealed stair down to the Temple of Urgathoa. Confronted, he plays the gracious host and tries to talk the PCs away; the moment he senses an attack he turns invisible and flees below.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Reiner Davaulus (9)}
Reiner DavaulusModerate · 80 XP @ L7

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Davaulus} (bane-rapier Sneak Attacks, Inspire) — usually backed by the F5/F7 staff if alarmed. He flees to the Temple rather than die in the open.

His notes (here, or relocated to G14 if the PCs were fooled) confirm the manufactured plague. The stair down begins the Temple of Urgathoa.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Temple of Urgathoa — p.118.

G. The Temple of Urgathoa — the cult's hidden warren beneath the hospice, where blood veil is brewed and the dying are 'harvested.' Andaisin pre-buffs her lieutenants (status) each morning and rallies the temple the moment the alarm sounds.

Staffed by zealous @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{cultists of Urgathoa} (and the negotiable nosferatu @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska} in his lab). Stealth and speed matter — a roused temple converges on the Inner Sanctum.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G2 — p.118.

The temple's first defended threshold. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{Cultists} (and any Gray Maidens who followed Davaulus down) hold the entry and raise the alarm.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{Cultist of Urgathoa (3)}
Temple guardsTrivial · 30 XP @ L7

3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{cultists of Urgathoa} — scythes and void-channeling; they fight to the death.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G5 — p.120.

A blood-slick surgery where the cult vivisects victims to study the disease.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{Cultist of Urgathoa (3)}
Cult surgeonsTrivial · 20 XP @ L7

2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{cultists} mid-operation.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G7 — p.122.

The cultists' barracks. If the alarm is up, expect the off-duty zealots here to reinforce elsewhere.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{Cultist of Urgathoa (3)}
Off-duty cultTrivial · 40 XP @ L7

4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{cultists of Urgathoa}.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G8 — p.122.

Great vats where tainted blood is rendered into blood veil — the production heart of the plague. Destroying the vats (and the death's-head coffers' work) is a major blow.

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{Cultist of Urgathoa (3)}
Vat-tendersLow · 60 XP @ L7

6 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{cultists} tending the vats — a Severe swarm if met all at once; thin them in the approach.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G11 — p.124.

The nosferatu @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska Arkminos}'s imported laboratory, where he tests Varisian plague-immunity on the captive Ruan Mirukova (Deyanira's brother, very much alive).

Do not simply fight him. Ramoska is a CR-14-equivalent foe far above the party and wants no part of the cult's cause. He will sell Ruan and leave — for coin (~200 gp re-scaled), a magic item, a future favor, or the last death's head coffer from G14. Attacked, he fights savagely but Dimensional-Steps away rather than be destroyed.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska Arkminos (13)}
Ramoska Arkminos (only if forced)Extreme · 160 XP @ L7

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska} — Drain Life, Contagion, caustic bombs, fast healing 5, nosferatu resilience. An almost-unwinnable straight fight at this level; the intended path is the bargain.

Free Ruan (bargain or battle) to complete the Carowyn thread and earn the Mirukovas' lasting gratitude. Ramoska's lab is a trove of alchemical and disease lore (GM-scaled).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G13 — p.132.

The defiled approach to the Inner Sanctum, lined with Urgathoan iconography of feast and rot. Andaisin's last guards make their stand.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{Cultist of Urgathoa (3)}
Sanctum guardsTrivial · 20 XP @ L7

2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.urgathoaCultist1]{cultists} (one bearing Andaisin's status — her early warning).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, G14 — p.132.

The Inner Sanctum reeks of incense and corruption — an altar to the Pallid Princess slick with old blood, the last death's-head coffer upon it. Here Lady Andaisin awaits, scythe in hand, serene with terrible faith.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinUrgath01]{Lady Andaisin}, high priestess of Urgathoa, is the chapter's villain made flesh — and she does not die easily.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinUrgath01]{Lady Andaisin (9)}
BOSS — Lady AndaisinModerate · 80 XP @ L7

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinUrgath01]{Andaisin} buffs, air-walks aloft, and rains Slay Living / Greater Command / Harm before dropping into scythe range; she mists-and-heals when low.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinDaughtr1]{Andaisin, Daughter of Urgathoa (11)}
CLIMAX — Andaisin, Daughter of UrgathoaExtreme · 160 XP @ L7

The instant Andaisin dies, Urgathoa's blessing erupts: she rises as @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinDaughtr1]{a Daughter of Urgathoa} — a Large undead horror with reach claws, a devouring maw, a pestilent aura, and plague-bearing wounds. An Extreme-tier climax; the true end of Chapter 2.

On the altar: the final death's-head coffer (the plague-engine — Ramoska covets it; destroying it ends the seeding), Andaisin's +2 vicious scythe and +2 breastplate, a belt of mighty constitution, a cloak of resistance, a headband of wisdom, and the cult's hoard (re-scaled).

With Andaisin destroyed and the vats and coffer ended, the manufactured plague's source is broken — but per the persistent-plague overlay (Conversion Guide → Blood Veil), the cult's re-seeding only suppresses, and the Epidemic Clock continues until Ileosa falls in Chapter 6.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.2, Chapter Conclusion — p.132.

Chapter Conclusion. The Hospice and Temple fall, Davaulus and Andaisin are dead (or fled), and Korvosa's acute outbreak breaks. The PCs should be 8th level. But the victory is bittersweet — the city is scarred, the Gray Maidens entrenched, and Old Korvosa now sealed under quarantine.

Into Chapter 3. The crafted cure suppresses rather than eradicates (persistent-plague overlay): set the Epidemic Clock from the Survivor Count tally across the missions. The quarantine of Old Korvosa and the captured seneschal Neolandus (held by the Arkonas) launch 'Escape from Old Korvosa.' If Davaulus was duped-and-fled, the Red Mantis now hunt the PCs there.

Threads forward: the persistent plague + Epidemic Clock · the Gray Maidens (now a standing enemy) · Rolth's vendetta and his immunity research · Ramoska loose with his bargain (or destroyed) · Trinia & Vencarlo · the Arkonas + Neolandus → Chapter 3.
